title: Dogfood harnesses still call retired extract/review contracts
description: scripts/dogfood-rc2-archive.mjs still calls extractKnowledge with legacy source_session, while the current MCP/schema contract requires non-empty source_sessions[] and the tool handler re-parses to reject missing arrays (packages/shared/src/schemas/api-contracts.ts:646; packages/server/src/tools/extract-knowledge.ts:49). Related dogfood review/doctor harnesses still hard-code project-local .fabric/knowledge pending/canonical paths (scripts/dogfood-rc3-review.mjs:62, scripts/dogfood-rc4-doctor.mjs:155), but current write-side code says knowledge writes are store-only with no dual-root fallback (packages/server/src/services/cross-store-write.ts:43). This is distinct from registered doc/test-seed drift because these are executable scripts that can silently produce empty-session idempotency keys or fail against store-only path validation when re-run.
fix_direction: Retire or quarantine the rc2/rc3/rc4 dogfood harnesses as historical evidence, or update them to use source_sessions: [SOURCE_SESSION], fab_review-returned store pending_path values, and resolved write-target store paths; add a small contract test or README note so legacy harnesses are not treated as current runtime validation.

context.notes: Discovered by testing-quality issue discovery DBP-20260609-011712

issue_history.note: Discovered by testing-quality issue discovery DBP-20260609-011712

title: fabric onboard-coverage tests do not exercise the mounted-store user workflow
description: The public onboard-coverage command resolves the current project's mounted store read-set before scanning canonical knowledge (packages/cli/src/commands/onboard-coverage.ts:178 and :203), but its tests import only runOnboardCoverage and seed files under project-local .fabric/knowledge (packages/cli/__tests__/onboard-coverage.test.ts:23 and :51). The shared resolver/builders show the real workflow depends on global/project store config and cross-store reads (packages/shared/src/store/resolve-input.ts:20; packages/shared/src/store/core.ts:134), so the current suite covers parser/helper fragments rather than the user-visible mounted-store workflow. This is distinct from registered store-only docs/runtime drift because the issue is missing integration coverage for the command's true read-set path.
fix_direction: Replace or supplement the local .fabric/knowledge fixtures with an integration fixture that creates a global Fabric config, mounts a store with store.json, binds it in project fabric-config.json, writes canonical knowledge/<type> entries in that store, and invokes onboardCoverageCommand with --json/human output assertions.

context.notes: Discovered by testing-quality issue discovery DBP-20260609-011712

issue_history.note: Discovered by testing-quality issue discovery DBP-20260609-011712

title: Install v2 HooksStage error results are untested and still reported as completed
description: HooksStage catches step failures into InstallStepResult objects with status="error" at packages/cli/src/install/pipeline/hooks.stage.ts:134 and :151, collects them into an errors array at :111, but then ignores that array and returns stageRan("hooks") at :117. Registry search found no duplicate, and tests do not reference HooksStage; packages/cli/__tests__/install-v2-pipeline.test.ts covers only guidance/env/store happy paths, so no negative test proves a failing hook/config/bootstrap step makes the pipeline fail instead of showing install success.
fix_direction: Add a HooksStage unit/integration test that forces one best-effort and one single-step installer to fail, then assert the stage or pipeline returns disposition='failed' with errors; change HooksStage to return stageFailedFromError or an explicit failed StageResult when installResults contains any status='error'.

context.notes: Discovered by testing-quality issue discovery DBP-20260609-011712

issue_history.note: Discovered by testing-quality issue discovery DBP-20260609-011712

title: Install v2 PreflightStage writable and git negative paths are empty and uncovered
description: PreflightStage documents and calls write-permission and git-availability checks at packages/cli/src/install/pipeline/preflight.stage.ts:40-45, but assertWritable and assertGitAvailable are empty methods at :93 and :98, while assertGlobalRootWritable only assumes an existing directory is writable at :87. Registry/test search found no PreflightStage, assertWritable, or assertGitAvailable tests, so unwritable targets/global roots and missing git for --url are not exercised before later stages perform writes or clone operations.
fix_direction: Implement the preflight contracts with fs access/temp-file checks for target/global-root writability and a mocked spawn/exec git probe for --url, then add tests for unwritable target, global root as file/unwritable directory, no HOME/USERPROFILE, and missing git returning a failed preflight StageResult.

context.notes: Discovered by testing-quality issue discovery DBP-20260609-011712

title: CLI i18n snapshot pins stale Cursor hook/skill capability output
description: The install i18n snapshot locks the Cursor row as `hook=n/a`, `skill=n/a`, and `manual step required`, while the current installer writes Cursor hook config/bootstrap and the parity matrix explicitly says Cursor has full skill/hook/mcp support. The snapshot test only accepts the full sanitized stdout, so updating it with `vitest -u` can continue protecting the stale capability presentation instead of asserting the semantic contract that installed Cursor assets must be reflected in the capability summary. This is distinct from the registered Cursor docs drift: it is a test/fixture baseline that preserves an incorrect runtime capability display.
fix_direction: Replace the broad stdout snapshot for the capability table with semantic assertions over parsed rows, and fix `detectClientSupports` so Cursor reports hook/bootstrap assets after install; keep the snapshot only for locale copy once the row semantics are asserted.

context.notes: Discovered by testing-quality issue discovery DBP-20260609-011712

issue_history.note: Discovered by testing-quality issue discovery DBP-20260609-011712

title: MCP HTTP initialize success path is claimed but unconditionally skipped
description: The HTTP integration test header claims coverage for the MCP HTTP transport initialize handshake, but the only test that asserts a successful initialize/session-creation response is an unconditional it.skip. The nearby 400/404 cases exercise non-initialize failure branches only, so the success path can regress while the suite remains green. This is distinct from existing ISS-20260608-042, which is about the retired /api/intent/annotate header entry, not this skipped MCP initialize guard.
fix_direction: Replace the skipped test with a runnable isolated initialize handshake test by providing the required server/tool-registration environment or factoring createSession behind an injectable adapter; if the path remains intentionally out of scope, remove the header claim and add an explicit tracked TODO/issue instead of it.skip.

context.notes: Discovered by testing-quality issue discovery DBP-20260609-011712

issue_history.note: Discovered by testing-quality issue discovery DBP-20260609-011712

title: CLI and server coverage gates are below the project target
description: Root test:coverage delegates to package coverage scripts, but the CLI package accepts 70% lines/statements and the server package accepts 75%, below the project-level 80%+ testing standard. A broad coverage gate can therefore pass while two primary packages are under the expected minimum; shared already uses 85%, so this is not a repo-wide technical limitation.
fix_direction: Raise CLI and server coverage thresholds to at least 80% lines/statements, or document a temporary exception with a burn-down target and package-specific missing-coverage list; keep the root coverage script as the enforcing entrypoint.

context.notes: Discovered by testing-quality issue discovery DBP-20260609-011712
